Project Lead & Department Secretary · IVA

I make things
happen.

Twelve years at one of Sweden's most respected institutions, working where entrepreneurship, innovation and real-world execution meet — and increasingly drawn to the question of how technology moves from hype into something that actually creates value.

EVENTS PEOPLE AI

I'm at my best in roles where I get to bring people together, build trust, shape a plan, and help ideas turn into something real. Underneath all of it, the work is the same: being the translator between people and technology.

I

How I work

Design the conversation first

Themes, workshops, headlines, agendas — and above all, getting the right people in the room to discuss the right thing. The hardest, highest-value work happens long before the doors open.

The room is the product

Staging, signal flow and timing are designed backwards from the feeling you want at minute 90 — not forwards from a checklist.

People before programs

A nervous keynote, a chairperson who needs talking points, a technician with ten seconds to react — my job is making each of them succeed.

Automate the second time

Anything done twice becomes a system. Human attention goes where machines can't: the room, the relationships, the judgement calls.

II

Flagship productions

Project Lead · 2022, 23, 25, 26

IVA's Annual Ceremony

IVA's most formal evening — the Högtidssammankomst in Stockholm City Hall's Golden Hall, with ceremony, banquet and ~1,200 guests in white tie. Project lead for the production.

Production & Technical Director · 2024–26

Entrepreneurship Summit

IVA's flagship summit bringing together founders, investors and decision-makers. Responsible for production and technical direction across the whole event.

Co-producer · since 2019

Prince Daniel's Entrepreneurship Day

The Fellowship's national gathering for young entrepreneurs, with HRH Prince Daniel. Co-producer of the day — stage, run-of-show and production.

Project Lead · since 2018

Prince Daniel's Fellowship

Entreprenörssamtal, Entreprenörsresor and school visits across Sweden — leading the production of the Fellowship's outreach programme, often with HRH Prince Daniel.

→ See the full event archive — 134 events
III

The AI layer

My background isn't the classic one. I haven't spent the last ten years calling myself an AI consultant or pretending to have all the answers. What I've done is wrestle with it in practice — tools, workflows, local models, agents — trying to understand what's actually useful, where the real friction is, and what separates genuine value from empty promises.

Some of that has been exciting. Some of it has felt a bit like voluntarily chewing glass. But when something finally clicks, you get a glimpse of what's possible, and it becomes very hard to look away. I run WhenIPO, a private-markets tracker I built and operate myself — and the site you're reading renders itself from a structured database of my own work.

agentic workflowsretrievalstructured outputslocal modelsprompt iterationdata-driven sites
